commits.author: Jérémy Franck
commits.subject: refactor(Builder): centralize AI Assistant availability checks
commits.body:
Add a shared `canUseAiAssistant()` helper to the Builder settings store and use it anywhere the UI needs to decide whether AI Assistant features should be
available.

This replaces repeated availability checks in the selected element overlay, structure context menu, AI chat screen element, and AI settings panel with one
shared predicate. The goal is to keep all AI entry points aligned with the same definition of availability.

Testing strategy:
- add focused unit coverage for `canUseAiAssistant()` in `Settings.svelte.test.ts`
- keep consumer tests focused on consumer behavior by mocking `canUseAiAssistant()` directly in:
  - `SelectedBlockOverlay.test.ts`
  - `structureUtils.test.ts`

The goal is to keep the helper logic verified in one place while avoiding repeating settings/feature-flag wiring details in every downstream test.
Consumer tests now assert only what those components care about: whether AI controls are shown or hidden based on the helper result.

commits.author: Jérémy Franck
commits.subject: fix(Builder): hide AI context controls when AI Assistant is disabled
commits.body:
Prevent AI context actions from appearing when the AI Assistant setting/feature flag is off.

This keeps the selected-element overlay and structure menu aligned with the actual availability of AI features and avoids exposing a
dead UI affordance.

ETC-622.title: Regression bug for HTML in HTML Block
ETC-622.description:
Our etch specific HTML Elements do not render correctly in the HTML Editor if the parent is a plain HTML Element and is selected:

Quick vid from jeremy:
[https://kommodo.ai/recordings/W0DsDsbeTluw4BhoeQck](<https://kommodo.ai/recordings/W0DsDsbeTluw4BhoeQck>)

ETC-617.title: Redesign prop key display in component editor
ETC-617.description:
When "show keys" mode is active in the component editor, the current two-column layout (prop name + truncated key) wastes space and truncates keys. Redesign to a single-column layout where the full key takes the entire row width, with a copy button on hover.

Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] In key display mode, each property row shows only the full key (e.g., {services.booleanProp}) - prop name is hidden
- [ ] Keys are never truncated, they use the full available row width
- [ ] On hover, a copy-to-clipboard button appears on the right side of the row
- [ ] Clicking the copy button copies the key string (including braces) to the clipboard

![image.png](https://uploads.linear.app/48a03f57-ea0e-4dba-b596-2bf09659f4d1/3cd3e9e3-3fc7-4747-a364-bfde6bdb337e/d7c71c21-b5b4-4f7c-9f76-c75cd0f161bb)

ETC-613.title: Strip curly braces when pasting into condition prop input
ETC-613.description:
AC:

* When the user pastes dynamic data wrapped in curly braces e.g. `{props.show}` into the input for the condition prop the curly braces will be stripped
* When the user pastes dynamic data not-wrapped in curly braces e.g. `props.show` into the input for the condition prop, it will just paste as normal
* Paste of plain text should be untouched
